title: Cell lysis and gel electrophoresis for protein analysis of HeLa cells
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"1aau","text":"Here, we present multiple protocols used for biochemical analysis of protein expression and association. First, we used a simple lysis technique to determine the efficiency of an siRNA knockdown. Then, we modified two previously published methods for assaying co-precipitation of p62 and NEMO with magnetic beads conjugated to a GFP-trap molecule. In the first, we pulled down EGFP-NEMO in control or mitochondrial damaged conditions, and in the second, we pulled down EGFP-Ubiquitin in p62-/- cells with expression of wild-type p62 or a dysfunctional mutant. Since p62 is known to form multimers, we used specialized buffers to preserve those putative interactions. We were able to reproduce results published previously by pulling down EGFP-Ubiquitin in p62-expressing cells. However, interestingly, we did not find evidence that NEMO interacts with p62 in the soluble fraction, or via ubiquitin chains generated in basal conditions. These studies demonstrated that NEMO recruitment to damaged mitochondria occurs in specific circumstances, and NEMO colocalization with p62 is also dependent on multiple factors. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Aspirate media from dishes.

Wash samples quickly x2 with ice cold PBS. Can stop after washes by scraping cells with the second wash of PBS into 1.5 mL tube, spin down at 2400 x g, °C, , 00:03:00 , 4 °C . Aspirate PBS and snap-freeze tubes in liquid nitrogen. If frozen, add respective lysis buffer and inhibitors and let thaw On ice 00:10:00 before proceeding.

We used this protocol to assess depletion of p62 in HeLa-M cells after siRNA treatment and imaging NEMO recruitment. Samples were collected from 35 mm imaging dishes. Add 150 µL RIPA + inhibitors to dish and scrape cells into 1.5 uL tube, OR add buffer to thawed sample and resuspend by pipetting.
Rotate resuspended sample on end-over-end machine at 4 °C for 00:20:00 .
Spin at top speed ( 17000 x g, °C, ), 4 °C , 00:20:00 .
Remove supernatant as Lysis and keep On ice or store at -80 °C .

Measure protein concentration with Pierce BCA assay by adding 25 µL sample or BSA standard to each well in duplicate and 200 µL Reagent A+B. Incubate 37 °C for 00:30:00 then measure absorbance on a plate reader. It is likely necessary to dilute samples 1:4 or more to measure within the range of the assay.
Add 1/3 volume of 4X DB to remaining Lysis or a measured fraction of sample and heat 95 °C for 00:05:00 .
Proceed to gel electrophoresis.

The samples for this assay are: A) EGFP-vector + veh, B) EGFPvector + AntA/OligA, C) EGFP-NEMO + veh, and D) EGFP-NEMO + AntA/OligA. Start with 3x 10 cm dish per condition for one biological replicate.
18- 24 hr before collection, transfect 3 µg Parkin and 1.5 µg EGFPNEMO (or vector), to 70-80% confluent cells on each dish.
It is best to plan collection times such that each is collected and snap frozen ~ 00:10:00 apart. Then all snap frozen samples can be processed simultaneously from that step.
Scrape and combine like samples into 800 µL PBS, transfer to 1.5mL tube.
Aspirate PBS and snap freeze in liquid nitrogen.
Add 800 µL LB-A with inhibitors to each frozen sample.
Pipet to resuspend.
Incubate On ice 00:10:00 .
Centrifuge at 1000 x g, °C, , 00:05:00 , 4 °C .
Transfer sup. (Cytosol) to a new tube.
Centrifuge again at top speed, 00:10:00 , 4 °C .
Save pellet as Heavy Fraction if desired and resuspend in 50 µL 1X DB.
Mix GFP-Trap beads by swirling and gently pipetting to break up accumulations.
Aliquot 25 µL bead slurry into LoBind tubes (one for each condition).
Remove supernatant from beads by inserting the tube in a magnetic rack and carefully aspirating clear solution.
Resuspend beads in 500 µL cold PBST.
Insert to magnetic rack and remove PBST wash.
Block beads in 2% BSA (in water) for 01:00:00 at 4 °C with end-over-end rotation.
Wash 2X with 500 µL Room temperature PBST.
Wash 1X with 500 µL LB-A.
Allow magnets to collect beads for up to 00:05:00 .
Add 500 µL Cytosol to bead tube, rotate end-over-end gently for 01:00:00 , 4 °C (save the rest of Cytosol for Input).
Remove supernatant by magnet (up to 00:05:00 ) and save as Flow Through.
Wash 3X with 500 µL Room temperature . PBS (no TWEEN) (can save these washes).
For the last wash, transfer beads and wash to a new tube. Optional if using Lo-bind tubes.
Remove sup from last wash and resuspend in ~ 90 µL 1X DB with rough mixing (vortex, pipetting), then heat 95 °C for 00:05:00 .
Magnetize 00:05:00 and save sup as Eluate.
Add 1/3 volume of 4X DB to Input and Flow Through and heat 95 °C for 00:05:00 .
Proceed to gel electrophoresis.

The samples for this assay are in p62-/- Hela cells: A) EGFP-vector + mCherry-vector, B) EGFP-vector + mCherry-p62, C) EGFPUbiquitin + mCherry-vector, D) EGFP-Ubiqutin + mCherry-p62, and E) EGFP-Ubiquitin + mCherry-p62-ΔPB1. Start with 2x 10 cm dish per condition for one biological replicate.

18- 24 hr before collection, transfect 3 µg Parkin and EGFPUbiquitin (or vector), 1.5 µg NEMO, and 2 µg - 2.5 µg mCherry-p62 (or vector) to 70-80% confluent cells in each dish. It is best to plan collection times such that each is collected and snap frozen ~ 00:10:00 apart. Then all snap frozen samples can be processed simultaneously from that step.
Combine samples by scraping cells into 600 µL LB-B with inhibitors and transfer to 1.5 mL tube.
Incubate On ice , 00:15:00 .
Transfer supernatant to a new tube as Lysis.
Mix GFP-Trap beads by swirling or gently pipetting to break up accumulations and aliquot 10 µL into LoBind tubes (one for each condition).
Remove supernatant from beads by inserting the tube in a magnetic rack and carefully aspirating clear solution.
Resuspend beads in 500 µL Room temperature Wash Buffer.
Insert to magnetic rack and remove wash.
Repeat for a total of 2 washes.
Wash 1X with 500 µL LB-B.
Allow magnets to collect beads for up to 00:05:00 .
Add 500 µL Lysed sample to bead tube, rotate gently for 01:00:00 , Room temperature .
Save the rest of the Lysis as Input.
Remove supernatant by magnet (up to 00:05:00 ) and save as Flow Through.
Wash 3X with 500 µL Room temperature Wash buffer (can save these washes).
For the last wash, transfer beads and wash to a new tube (optional if you’re using Lo-bind tubes).
Remove supernatant from last wash and resuspend in ~ 90 µL 1X DB with rough mixing (vortex, pipetting), then heat block 00:10:00 .
Magnetize 00:05:00 to collect beads and save supernatant as Eluate.
Add 1/3 volume of 4X DB to Input and Flow Through and heat 95 °C for 00:05:00 .
Proceed to gel electrophoresis.
This protocol was developed for use with the LI-COR system for protein detection.
Set up electrophoresis cell with 10% gels by manufacturer’s instructions.
Fill cell with RB and flush wells with a plastic transfer pipet.
Invert samples by hand to mix, and ensure all samples are at the bottom of tubes by briefly centrifuging.
Load wells with equal amounts of protein (Option 1) or equal volumes (Options 2 and 3) and molecular weight standard ( 4 µL - 5 µL ). We load 15 µL - 25 µL eluate and 10 µL Input.
For empty lanes, load approx. equal volume of 1X DB.
Run samples through stacking gel (85 V, 00:20:00 - 00:40:00 ).
Run samples through 10% gel (125 V, until front has reached bottom of gel, usually ~ 01:10:00 ).
Remove gels from electrophoresis cell and construct transfer cassettes with PVDF membranes according to manufacturer’s instructions.
Place the cassettes in the transfer cell and fill cell with Transfer buffer and icepack.
Place the cell in a basin.
Fill basin with ice around cell.
Run transfer for 01:00:00 - 01:10:00 , 100 V.
Dry membrane between filter paper in the dark for at least 00:45:00 . This is most important for small proteins. Can be a stopping point for several days.
Rehydrate membrane in MetOH.
Wash in ddWater.
Wash in 1X TBS 00:02:00 .
Stain total protein, 00:05:00 , Room temperature , with REVERT 700 Total Protein Stain.
Wash membrane 2x 00:00:30 with REVERT wash buffer.
Image total protein on LICOR. Can cut the membrane based on total stain if desired.
Rinse in ddWater.
Block membranes in EveryBlot buffer, 00:05:00 , Room temperature with rocking.
Incubate in vacuum packs with primary antibodies in EveryBlot Overnight at 4 °C . See materials and methods for concentrations of antibodies used.
Wash with TBST.
Incubate with secondary antibody 1:20,000 in TrueBlack antibody diluent with 0.2% TWEEN and 1:1000 10% SDS for up to 01:00:00 .
Wash with TBST.
Wash 1X with TBS to clear TWEEN.
Wash 1X with water.
Image
For quantification of knockdown (Option 1).
Use ImageStudio software to draw rectangles around total protein in each lane of Total Protein image and subtract background.
Then add rectangles to outline p62 bands and subtract background.
Transfer intensity measurements to Excel.
Calculate p62 expression relative to total protein for each experiment.
For quantification of p62 enrichment from GFP-NEMO immunoprecipitation.
Use ImageStudio to add rectangles around input/cytosolic p62 and eluted p62.
Transfer intensity measurements with background subtracted to Excel.
Calculate p62 eluted relative to input amounts.

Calculate elution/input quantity relative one condition. We found it necessary to perform this secondary normalization due to variability across replicates. In our case, we normalized results from each condition to the EGFP-NEMO + AntA/OligA results.
